title: rhinanthus serotinus subsp. polycladus
intro: Rhinanthus serotinus subsp. polycladus, also known as the pale bee orchid or pale monkey flower, is a rare and endangered subspecies of the serotine broomrape plant, a type of parasitic flowering plant that obtains its nutrients by attaching itself to the roots of host plants. Traditionally, Rhinanthus serotinus subsp. polycladus has been used in herbal medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The plant is believed to have several health benefits, including reducing fever and relieving digestive issues, although more research is needed to confirm its effectiveness. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used in teas, tinctures, and infusions, although it's worth noting that its availability and use may be restricted due to its rarity and endangered status.
